questions.promptText: Hi sweetheart, is this a bad time?
questions.followupText: I haven't heard from you all week.

responses.text: Never for you, Mom.
Kind of, actually. Can I call you back?
No! I was literally just thinking about you.
Oh you know. How are you?
responses.followupQuestion: You're always so busy. Is everything actually okay?

followupResponses.text: Yes, I'm fine. Just a lot going on.
Honestly, it's been a weird few weeks.
Mom, you're the one who called me.
I don't know. A lot of things at once. It'll settle.

questions.promptText: Okay, I'll let you go — just tell me one real thing.
questions.followupText: Just one. I won't panic, I promise.

responses.text: I'm okay. I really am.
responses.followupQuestion: Okay. I believe you. Call me this weekend?

followupResponses.text: Of course. Sunday?
Already in my calendar.
I'll try. Work's been unpredictable.
I need a few days first. I'll call when I'm ready.

responses.text: I miss you.
I have a plan. Kind of. The plan has a plan.
I love you, Mom.

questions.promptText: Did you eat something today? A real meal, not coffee?
questions.followupText: You always forget to eat when you're stressed.

responses.text: I had eggs. With toast. I'm fine, Mom.
...I had like half a granola bar?
I'm literally eating right now. Chewing. Hear it?
Can we not start with this?

questions.promptText: You sound tired. Are you sleeping?
questions.followupText: I know you. I can hear it in your voice.

responses.text: Great, actually. Best sleep of my life.
Not really. I was up until four.
responses.followupQuestion: Four in the morning. Doing what?

followupResponses.text: Working. There's a deadline I didn't mention.
I couldn't sleep. My head was just... loud.
Scrolling, mostly. It's a bad habit. I know.
I don't remember. Just awake.

responses.text: Sleep is complicated. But fine. You know. Fine.
responses.followupQuestion: Fine as in fine or fine as in you don't want to talk about it?

followupResponses.text: Both, kind of. I don't know how to explain it.
Fine as in fine. Stop reading into it.
Can we not do this? It's fine.
The second one. I really don't want to talk about it.

responses.text: You sound tired too. Are YOU sleeping?

questions.promptText: How's the apartment? Is it... okay?
questions.followupText: I just want to be able to picture where you live.

responses.text: It's great. Cozy. Very mine.
responses.followupQuestion: And when's the last time you cleaned it?

followupResponses.text: Last weekend, actually.
I just cleaned it. Like, this week.
It's not that kind of mess.
I don't remember. It's been a while.

responses.text: The bathroom situation is... evolving.
responses.followupQuestion: What does evolving mean? Evolving like how?

followupResponses.text: The tile situation is being... negotiated.
There's a leak. It's small. The landlord knows.
I meant it's getting better. Actively evolving toward good.
I'm dealing with the landlord. It's in progress.

responses.text: I literally just deep-cleaned it. Ask anyone.
responses.followupQuestion: Who is anyone.

followupResponses.text: My roommate. She was literally there.
Okay, fine. It's a little chaotic right now.
My neighbor. She has a key.
People who've been over. People.

responses.text: Why? What did you hear?
responses.followupQuestion: I heard something. From your cousin.

followupResponses.text: She's exaggerating. You know how she is.
What did she say exactly?
That was weeks ago. It's fine now.
Cousins talk. It's nothing.

questions.promptText: Have you seen any of your friends lately?
questions.followupText: You seemed a little lonely last time we talked.

responses.text: Yeah, we got together last week. It was really nice.
I've been kind of... in my head lately.
responses.followupQuestion: In your head how? Are you okay?

followupResponses.text: Yes. Processing some things. I'm fine.
Honestly? Kind of lonely. But it passes.
Just work stuff. Nothing you need to worry about.
It's anxiety. It comes and goes. I manage it.

responses.text: Tons. Literally so many friend activities. I'm exhausted from all the socializing.
responses.followupQuestion: Are you okay? Really?

followupResponses.text: Honestly? A little stretched. But I'm managing.
I'm great. I love being busy.
Can we change the subject?
Not really. I think I overcommitted myself.

responses.text: People are busy. It's fine.

questions.promptText: How's work? Are they treating you well?
questions.followupText: I just want to know someone's looking out for you.

responses.text: It's great. I'm really hitting my stride.
responses.followupQuestion: Your stride. What does that look like, exactly?

followupResponses.text: More clients, better projects. It's clicking.
Keeping busy. Staying relevant. You know.
Honestly I just mean I'm not panicking every day.
I mean things are moving. It's hard to explain.

responses.text: I've been freelancing a bit. Exploring options.
responses.followupQuestion: Exploring options means what, exactly? Do you have income coming in?

followupResponses.text: I have a few clients. It's picking up.
Some months are better than others.
Mom, I'm figuring it out. That's the whole point.
Some months are better than others. This one's been hard.

responses.text: They actually gave me more responsibility. Unofficially. It's a thing.
Work is work. You know how it is.

questions.promptText: Are you keeping up with bills? Just be honest.
questions.followupText: I found a check I wrote you last year. It was never cashed.

responses.text: Completely on top of it. Autopay, even.
I'm a little behind on one thing. It's manageable.
responses.followupQuestion: What does manageable mean? Which bill?

followupResponses.text: Electric. One month. I have it covered next week.
It's handled. I don't want to get into it.
It's more like... a few things, actually. Smaller things.
I'm figuring it out. I always do.

responses.text: Absolutely. I also set up a budget spreadsheet. It has color coding.
The economy is insane right now. Even for people who are doing fine.
responses.followupQuestion: The economy is insane. And yet rent is still due.

followupResponses.text: I know. I'm on top of it. Barely.
I'm a little behind. I'm working on it.
I set up autopay last week. It's fine.
I know, Mom. I'm aware.

questions.promptText: Are you seeing anyone? You haven't mentioned anyone in a while.
questions.followupText: It's okay if there's nobody. I just want to know.

responses.text: I'm focusing on myself right now. Very intentionally.
Actually, yeah. It's new. I didn't want to jinx it.
responses.followupQuestion: What's their name? What do they do?

followupResponses.text: Alex. They work in design.
Jamie. Something in tech. I'm not sure exactly.
I'm not doing this. It's too new.
We haven't talked about what we are yet.

responses.text: No. Definitely not. One hundred percent single. Why?
Mom, can we not do this right now?

questions.promptText: I thought I heard another voice in the background. Is someone there?
questions.followupText: I'm not upset. I just want to know.

responses.text: That's the TV. I leave it on for company. Very normal.
That's my roommate. We split the place.
responses.followupQuestion: And you didn't tell me? Is it a nice person?

followupResponses.text: Yeah, I should have. They're great, actually.
I didn't think it was a big deal.
It happened fast. I'll explain more later.
I didn't want to hear your opinion on it.

responses.text: It was a podcast! A podcast where someone talks! Very normal podcast!
The walls are thin. Neighbors.
responses.followupQuestion: It sounded like it was inside the room.

followupResponses.text: The acoustics are weird. Old building.
Okay — I had someone over.
My laptop mic. It picks up everything.
Mom, you're analyzing my audio now?

questions.promptText: Your cousin said you've been Venmoing her for coffee. Multiple times.
questions.followupText: She wasn't gossiping. She was worried.

responses.text: We have a reciprocal coffee situation. It's a whole thing.
I've been a little tight this month. It's not a big deal.
responses.followupQuestion: A little tight. Since when?

followupResponses.text: A couple of months. But it's stabilizing.
Just this month. I have something coming in soon.
It's just how it is right now.
Since things changed. I'll explain when I can.

responses.text: I Venmo people all the time. That's just friendship now.
responses.followupQuestion: She told me because she was worried. That makes two of us.

followupResponses.text: She worries about everything. It's nothing.
Okay, I've been helping her out. Mutually.
She completely misread the situation.
You don't have to worry. Either of you.

responses.text: She told you that? What else did she tell you?

questions.promptText: I just need to know you have some kind of plan. Any plan.
questions.followupText: Not a big plan. Even a small one.

responses.text: I have a five-year plan. It's very detailed.
responses.followupQuestion: A five-year plan. Can I hear it?

followupResponses.text: It's still in progress. I'll send you the overview.
It's more like a direction than a plan, honestly.
It's complicated. There are a lot of variables.
I don't actually have one. I said it and it sounded good.

responses.text: I have... the beginning of a plan.
I have seventeen plans. The problem is just choosing.
responses.followupQuestion: Seventeen. And which one is winning?

followupResponses.text: The practical one. I think.
None of them yet. That's kind of the problem.
It depends on the day. Today? I don't know.
The one where I figure it out as I go.

responses.text: Plans are for people who know what they want. I'm still becoming.

questions.promptText: Your cousin said she saw you at the mall. On Tuesday. At 2pm.

responses.text: Client meeting. We walked and talked.
Okay. I got let go last month.
responses.followupQuestion: Oh, sweetheart. How long ago? Why didn't you call me?

followupResponses.text: I needed time to process it first.
I was handling it. I didn't want to worry you.
I don't know. I just didn't.
Because I knew you'd panic. And I wasn't ready for that.

responses.text: Tell Rachel to mind her business.
I told you — they changed my hours. I work nights now.

questions.promptText: Are you and Marcus still... you said you broke up.
questions.followupText: I'm only asking because I liked him. Or I did.

responses.text: It's complicated. We're working on it.
No. We're completely done. That was final.
He needed to borrow the couch temporarily. It's very platonic.
Mom, I'm handling it.

questions.promptText: How's rent this month. Actually. Don't say fine.
questions.followupText: I can send you something if you need it.

responses.text: Covered. Already paid. Stop worrying.
I'm two weeks late but I talked to the landlord.
I sold something. Don't ask what. Rent is handled.
It's handled. Let's not do this.
responses.followupQuestion: It's not handled if you won't tell me how.

followupResponses.text: I got a little help. It's sorted.
I borrowed from a friend. I'll pay it back.
I sold some stuff. It covered it.
It's handled means it's handled, Mom.

questions.promptText: Do you ever think about going back to school? To finish?

responses.text: I've actually been looking into it.
No. That's not the path anymore. I've made peace with it.
I'm building something actually. It's hard to explain. It's kind of like a business.
Mom, can we talk about something else?

questions.promptText: When's the last time you went to the doctor? An actual doctor.

responses.text: I had a checkup like... recently. It was good.
responses.followupQuestion: Recently like last month, or the other kind of recently?

followupResponses.text: Like, this year. Definitely this year.
The other kind. But I feel fine.
I don't remember the exact date.
Okay. It's been a while. I've been avoiding it.

responses.text: I don't have insurance right now. I'm looking into it.
I've been very healthy. I take vitamins. I read about my body.
I feel great. That's what matters.
